Management informed the Audit Team that both the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal General Services Office have designated a specific personnel to prioritize the maintenance of inventory records and to secure storage of actual inventory items. Although they initially implemented measures to adhere the audit observations in Calendar Year (CY) 2024, their efforts were constrained by the dual responsibilities assigned to the concerned staff, who were simultaneously acquiring other pertinent functions. For the CY 2025, they have worked to systematize their inventory management procedures. With the clearer delineation of responsibilities and improved coordination between the two offices, they are hopeful of achieving full compliance by the end of CY 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the:
a. Municipal GSO/Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of
all PPE and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Accounting Office for
reconciliation with accounting records and the Audit Team not later than
January 31 of each year for audit purposes;
b. Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to:
1. Complete the details of the PPELCs and PCs for each category of asset and
reconcile both records;
2. Attend to the manpower needs in their respective Offices to manage PPE
owned and controlled by the Municipality;
3. Consider acquiring a computerized accounting system with the capability to
generate audit trail reports, valuable information and financial statements
such as the PPELCs, depreciation schedule and details in General Ledger, as
well as the ability to produce reports such as the Property Acknowledgement
Receipt (PAR) and PCs, among others, for easy identification and
reconciliation of records;
c. Municipal Agriculturists to expedite the completion of the supporting
documents and submit the same to the Audit Team together with the request for
relief from accountability. If not, demand payment from the person/s responsible
for the loss of work/zoo animals; and
d. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to exert extra effort to determine
the details of the unidentified PPE items totaling P66,509,276.83 following the
guidelines and procedures of the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account
Balances” as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
We also recommended that Management implement the “One-Time Cleansing of
PPE Account Balances” as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to
establish verifiable PPE balances as to accountability, condition and existence.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Immediately prepare and post the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
discrepancy identified during the reconciliation between the accounting
records and the Certified List of Delinquencies submitted by the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office; and
b. Ensure the timely posting of adjusting entries resulting from reconciliation
procedures and regularly coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to
ensure that RPT and SET Receivables and their related deferred revenue
accounts are accurately and fairly stated in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:
a. Municipal GSO to:
1. Conduct a physical count of inventories semestrally, prepare and submit
the RPCI to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each
year;
2. Ensure the proper maintenance and accurate recording of each type of
inventory in the Stock Card;
3. Prepare RIS for supplies and materials issued and consolidate all RIS using
the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same
to the Municipal Accounting Office;
4. Reconcile the Stock Card periodically with the Ledger Card of the Municipal
Accounting Office; and
5. Reconcile the RPCI with the General Ledger controlling accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant to:
1. Maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each commodity/stock and reconcile them
with the General Ledger controlling accounts;
2. Reconcile inventory accounts with RPCI; and
3. Draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments/corrections, if any.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to disclose the necessary supporting
information in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance with IPSAS for
the ensuing year.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant and/or Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Exert extra effort to speed up the retrieval of the supporting documents,
schedules, MOA and other records to properly maintain the required
Subsidiary Ledgers and establish the accuracy, existence and validity of these
accounts;
b. Immediately remit unexpended balances and savings from completed National
Government-funded projects to Source Agencies or the National Treasury and
submit the necessary liquidation reports; and
c. See to it that all accounts appearing in the financial statements are properly
supported with Subsidiary Ledgers and supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reclassify the noted Liabilities totaling P4,895,413.98 from the Accounts Payable
account to the appropriate Payables accounts in accordance with the Revised
Chart of Accounts for LGUs under COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
enforce strict compliance with PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2006-002 by
ensuring that all disbursements, including online payments, undergo proper
documentation and signatory approval.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
implement proper segregation of duties by ensuring that no individual is solely
responsible for both the collection and disbursement of funds to avoid exposure to
possible misappropriation of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the OIC – Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Budget Officer, ensure that the
20 percent DF is fully appropriated based on the actual NTA received and the amount
short of the required allocation be corrected through a supplemental appropriation.

We recommended that the Management, in coordination with the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (MDRRM) Officer, Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review the calculated 5 percent LDRRMF requirement based on the actual
income from regular sources for CY 2024 to ensure compliance with RA No.
10121;
b. Obtain the necessary resolutions from the MDRRM Council and Sangguniang
Bayan to formally support and authorize any adjustments made to the
LDRRMF appropriation;
c. Refrain from incurring and charging expenses that do not fall within the four
thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
d. Ensure strict compliance with the required five percent LDRRMF appropriation
in accordance with RA No. 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the noted discrepancy of P496,938.16 between the unutilized LDRRMF
reported in the NFS and the amount reflected as transferred to the Trust Fund in
the Statement of Financial Performance and prepare an adjusting entry, if
warranted.

Chairperson to include in the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report the
documents enumerated in Item 4.C.8(5) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-
01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, to facilitate the
assessment and evaluation of reported accomplishments.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Human Resources
Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to stop computing Government
Share for Pag-IBIG contributions based on the monthly basic salary and strictly
comply with the use of two percent of the P10,000.00 maximum fund salary limit as
reference in calculating the Government Share for Pag-IBIG contributions as
provided under RA No. 9679 and Pag-IBIG Circular No. 460 dated January 15,
2024.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the monthly trial balance, supported with the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations, and ensure delayed submission by the 20th day following the end of each month, as stipulated in Section 70 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b. Submit all monthly and quarterly reports, including the year-end FS and their supporting Notes to the FS and schedules, to the Audit Team within the stipulated timeframes; and
c. Collaborate with the Municipal Human Resources Management Office to propose a revised staffing pattern that addresses the inadequacy of the workforce in performing Accounting functions effectively.
observations.managementAction: A Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution dated July 10, 2024 was passed to formally assign the Municipal Administrator as the countersigning official for all disbursement of checks. One employee from the Municipal Accounting Office was promoted to a casual employee position to help address the workload and strengthen the financial reporting function. In addition, other accounting personnel were provided with necessary training and on-the-job experience to enhance their competency in preparing and submitting financial reports. As a result, the monthly and quarterly financial reports including the SAAOB, have since been submitted regularly. Most notably, the year-end Financial Statements for FY 2024 were submitted on February 14, 2025, demonstrating improved compliance.

b. Municipal Engineer/MPDC to strictly monitor the progress of work and facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2025, the MPDC conducted a more thorough review of project proposals by including only PPAs that were well-planned and implementation-ready in the AIP. For CY 2025, all approved 20 percent DF PPAs have specific implementation schedules incorporated in the AIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MDRRM Council to:
1. Explain/justify the incurrence of expenses charged from previous years' unexpended balance of LDRRMF despite not being included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP);
2. Include in the LDRRMFIP, under a separate caption, the list of PPAs funded from the STF; and
3. Closely monitor the implementation of the proposed PPAs and institutionalize measures to strengthen disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels to answer the needs of its constituents in times of calamities and disasters; and
b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC – MDRRMO to:
1. Take immediate corrective actions to address the identified discrepancies amounting to P1,695,614.20 and make necessary adjustments, if warranted, to ensure the accuracy of financial records; and
2. Implement measures to ensure accuracy and consistency between financial statements and utilization reports by conducting regular reconciliations and reviews to identify and rectify discrepancies promptly.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Council submitted a justification explaining that the formulation of the LDRRMFIP was not undertaken in the previous year due to the recent designation of a new Officer in Charge – Municipal Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (OIC-MDRRMO). The updated LDRRMFIP now includes a separate section identifying the PPAs financed through the STF. The Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO have addressed the identified discrepancies amounting to P1,695,614.20 through reconciliation of financial records and appropriate adjustments. Regular reconciliation procedures and enhanced monitoring mechanisms have been institutionalized to ensure the effective implementation of the proposed PPAs. Consequently, the Municipality was able to utilize 81.68 percent of the LDRRMF for CY 2025.

observations.managementAction:
The Appraisal Committee was created concurrently with the revision of the Inventory Committee in CY 2024 to perform functions such as the accurate valuation of lots.
The status of the applications for land titles of the Municipality remains unchanged, with only two out of all 30 applications being released in CY 2023. observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly implement the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 on all officials/personnel concerned and direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. submit all monthly/quarterly reports including the year-end Financial Statements and their supporting Notes to FS and schedules to the Audit Team within the required period; and b. determine the cause/s of the delay and initiate proper coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer and General Services Officer for possible updating of records of the Accounting Department in order to comply with the required dates of submission of accounts and reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. observations.recommendation:

PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of
each year;

b. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to complete the details of the PC and
PPELC for each category of asset and reconcile both records;

c. Municipal Agriculturist to expedite the completion of the supporting
documents enumerated in COA MC No. 92-571 and submit the same to the
Auditor together with the request for relief from accountability. If not, demand
payment from the person/s responsible for the loss of work animals; and

d. Municipal Accountant and the MGSO exert extra effort to determine the details
of the unidentified PPE items totaling P57,648,341.48 following the guidelines
and procedures of the “One-Tme Cleansing of PPE Accounts” as prescribed in
COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the Municipal Assessor to:

a.1 expedite the processing of the Transfer Certificate of Titles for the 30 lots
owned by the Municipal Government of Pakil to protect the interest of the
Municipality;

a.2 retrieve documents on one parcel of lot registered in the name of the
school for record purposes; and

a.3 reconcile the report with the Accounting records.

b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

b.1 coordinate with the Municipal Assessor for the reconciliation of their
records; and

b.2 secure documents from the Municipal Assessor as regards TCTs in the
name of the school for record purposes.

c. create an appraisal committee to reassess the lots owned by the Municipality
for proper recording and valuation of the Land account.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the MGSO to:

a.1 conduct physical count of inventories semestrally, generate/prepare and
submit the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCIs) to the
Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

a.2 properly maintain and observe correct recording in the SC for each type of
inventory;

a.3 prepare Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for supplies and materials issued
and consolidate all RIS using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) and submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office;

a.4 reconcile the SCs periodically with the ledger cards of the Office of the
Municipal Accountant; and

a.5 reconcile the RPCIs with the GL controlling accounts.

b. the Municipal Accountant to:

b.1 maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for each commodity/stock which is to be
reconciled with the GL controlling accounts;

b.2 reconcile Inventory accounts with RPCI; and

b.3 draw a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjustments/corrections, if any.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Accountant to:

a. exert extra effort to expedite the retrieval of the Memorandum of Agreement
(MOA) and other records to establish the details and purpose of the funds
received from National Government Agencies;

b. determine the details of the other accounts that remained unascertained;

c. see to it that all accounts appearing in the financial statements are properly
supported with subsidiary ledgers and supporting documents; and

d. remit all unexpended balances and savings from identified completed projects
funded by the National Government immediately to the source agencies.

a. Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS for all depository accounts to account
for the difference between the per book and per bank balances of the Cash in
Bank account amounting to P9,837,970.07;

discrepancy of P1,779,232.24 between their records pertaining to Cash in

adjustments; and

the end of each month to immediately establish and address the errors,
omissions and deficiencies in the Municipality’s transactions in compliance
with COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of PD. No. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to fast  track  the reconciliation of the   property  balances  per  books  and  RPCPPE,  and thereafter, make a regular reconciliation between their records.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct   the  Municipal Accountant to set up RPT and SET  Receivables at the  beginning of the year based on  the certified list of taxpayers and taxes due and collectible to be prepared and submitted by the Municipal  Treasurer  as provided under  Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I to be able to establish with certainty the RPT/SET Receivables.

We recommended that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  Municipal Planning Development Coordinator to see to it that  the  20%  Development Fund  be  optimally  utilized  to  help  achieve  desired  socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the general public.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the  Municipal  Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer  to submit to the Office of the Auditor the Report on Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th  day  after the end of each month using the  format  prescribed  under  Annex  B  of  COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Local School Board  refrain  from using SEF for purposes other than those authorized under Section 1 of  RA No. 5447, Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160 and Joint Circular No.  01, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998 in order not to  defeat  purpose  for which SEF was created  and  deplete  the  fund  for  authorized  SEF  programs,  projects.

We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  direct  the  Municipal Accountant to disclose the required financial information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with additional information value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the composition of the MSWMB complies with Section 5 Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Management Act of 2000 for effective formulation and implementation of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

    a) the Municipal Treasurer through the  Municipal Accountant to submit to the COA the 190 cancelled checks for validation;

    b) the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all  cancelled  checks  are attached to the  RCI  and reported chronologically with  the other checks issued and the word “Cancelled” shall be  indicated  in  the report; and

    c) the Disbursing Officer should  prepare checks with extra care;  and instruct payees of the proper handling of checks.
